taskDescription: Click on the key anatomical terms related to the knee joint and anterior leg in the text below.
textField:
The knee joint is the largest *synovial* joint in the body. It contains two *menisci* (medial and lateral) that deepen the tibial plateau and act as shock absorbers. The *cruciate* ligaments (*anterior* and *posterior*) are intracapsular but *extrasynovial* and prevent anteroposterior displacement of the tibia. The *medial* *collateral* ligament is attached to the medial *meniscus*, which is why it is commonly injured in the unhappy triad.

The anterior compartment of the leg contains *tibialis* anterior, extensor *hallucis* longus, extensor *digitorum* longus, and *peroneus* tertius. All are supplied by the *deep* *peroneal* nerve and *anterior* *tibial* artery. Injury to the deep peroneal nerve causes *foot* *drop* with loss of *dorsiflexion* and a characteristic high-stepping *steppage* gait.

The *dorsalis* *pedis* pulse is palpated on the dorsum of the foot. The *popliteus* muscle is the key to unlocking the knee from full extension. The *patella* is the largest *sesamoid* bone in the body, embedded within the *quadriceps* tendon.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review knee and anterior leg anatomy terminology.
Good effort! Some key terms were missed.
Excellent! You identified the key anatomical terms.
